chunks.content:
Article: Eva DVD image cropping
[]: Eva DVD image cropping             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Sun May 15, 2005 9:09 pm Hi. I'm really sorry if this has been discussed before, but has anyone noticed that the picture on the Evangelion DVDs looks cropped out on a 4:3 television but is fully displayed when played on a computer DVD player? (It's most apparent during some of the English episode title cards in the middle of the episodes - for example, the exclamation point in "Asuka STRIKES!" is not showing.)

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun May 15, 2005 11:04 pm Not having a DVD-Rom, I can't say anything about the frames appearing in their entirety, but I can say that the fact that eyecatches still appear to be grossly cropped on Platinum bothers me to death. Well, maybe not to death. It's disappointing, is what it is.

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Mon May 16, 2005 12:52 pm It's fine on a widescreen tv...

[]: Re: Eva DVD image cropping             Quote Post by KIC » Mon May 16, 2005 2:52 pm toiletduck wrote: Hi. I'm really sorry if this has been discussed before, but has anyone noticed that the picture on the Evangelion DVDs looks cropped out on a 4:3 television but is fully displayed when played on a computer DVD player? (It's most apparent during some of the English episode title cards in the middle of the episodes - for example, the exclamation point in "Asuka STRIKES!" is not showing.)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 16, 2005 3:55 pm TVs have "safe zones" around the edges, so you don't generally get to see the whole frame on normal TVs. It happens with just about all DVDs. There's nothing wrong with your DVDs.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon May 16, 2005 6:43 pm But the "safe zone" did not interfere with the picture in ADV's original DVD releases. There is definately less picture this time around. Shin-seiki special ordered the Renewal disks from Japan (before we knew there was going to be a Platinum) and they have the same problem. So

Article: Eva DVD image cropping
 the picture in ADV's original DVD releases. There is definately less picture this time around. Shin-seiki special ordered the Renewal disks from Japan (before we knew there was going to be a Platinum) and they have the same problem. So Gainax must have re-framed the digital masters in such a way that it caused the outer edges of the picture to intrude on the safe zone. This causes a BIG PROBLEM in EOE; in the scene towards the end where Rei is looking at Unit-01 in space, Rei is cropped out of the picture!
